name: Arrha
description_short: Arrha Credit Union, now a division of BrightBridge Credit Union, is a Massachusetts-based credit union offering checking, savings, loans, and mortgages to eligible members.
description_long:
Arrha Credit Union operated as an independent credit union serving members in Massachusetts before merging with BrightBridge Credit Union, the fifth largest credit union in Massachusetts with over $2.4 billion in assets and 120,000+ members. Under the merger agreement announced in 2025, Arrha continues to operate as a division of BrightBridge while maintaining familiar local branch presence and team members in the Springfield area. The transition is designed to be seamless, with existing members retaining their account numbers, routing numbers, and current loan terms through a phased system migration planned for Summer 2026.

Arrha offers comprehensive financial products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit, personal loans, mortgages, and credit cards. Existing members maintain access to Arrha-branded online and mobile banking platforms for their current accounts and loan products. New account applicants and those seeking new lending products or credit cards can apply through BrightBridge's online platform or any branch location. Business accounts and commercial lending remain managed by the Springfield area commercial team, preserving continuity for business customers.

The merger distinguishes Arrha by providing members with significantly expanded access—from limited branch locations to 19 additional BrightBridge branches across eastern Massachusetts and southern New Hampshire upon full system integration. Members gain access to BrightBridge's cutting-edge online and mobile banking experiences, a 24/7 call center, and a shared branching network while preserving their familiar local service experience. The company emphasizes member retention and continuity, explicitly maintaining all current loan rates, CD terms, and debit/ATM card functionality during the transition.

The primary caveat is that Arrha is in active merger transition, with significant system changes planned for Summer 2026. Existing members experience operational continuity now but should anticipate account number changes, platform consolidation, and potential service changes post-conversion. New members joining after January 1, 2026 will be onboarded directly into BrightBridge systems rather than Arrha platforms, creating a two-tiered member experience during the transition period. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Debit/ATM cards with international use capability
pros: No account number changes for existing members until Summer 2026 system conversion
Routing number (211885917) remains unchanged—no need to update direct deposit or bill payments
Expanded branch access to 19 additional locations after merger while maintaining familiar local branches
Existing loan rates and terms guaranteed unchanged through merger transition
Existing Arrha checks continue to be honored indefinitely
Access to 24/7 call center and shared branching network post-integration
Familiar local team members and Springfield area commercial lending continuity
cons: Major system conversion scheduled for Summer 2026 may disrupt banking services and account access temporarily
New members joining after January 1, 2026 cannot access Arrha mobile app; must use BrightBridge platform
Merger transition creates two-tiered member experience with different online banking platforms based on join date
Limited information provided about timeline for full merger completion and complete system integration
International debit card use requires advance phone notification rather than real-time online activation
best_for: Existing Arrha members seeking continuity during merger with minimal account disruption
Massachusetts and southern New Hampshire residents wanting expanded branch access post-merger
Business customers in Springfield area needing continuity of commercial lending relationships
Members prioritizing local service and familiar team members within a larger credit union network
diagnosis: Arrha is best for existing members seeking familiar credit union service with continuity during a merger transition, and for new Massachusetts/southern New Hampshire residents wanting access to expanded BrightBridge branch networks. The primary caveat is the major system conversion planned for Summer 2026, which may temporarily disrupt online banking and account access; new members should expect to use BrightBridge platforms rather than Arrha-branded services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
